title: WBTC Bridge - Open App
meta_description: wbtc bridge to the official Wrapped Bitcoin destination; verify WBTC on Ethereum, review bridge risks, and open app from the correct domain.
hero_h2: Move Bitcoin liquidity into Ethereum DeFi
hero_lead: wbtc bridge routes users toward the official Wrapped Bitcoin destination to verify WBTC on Ethereum, understand BTC mint and redeem paths, and open the app with the correct domain.
what_is: WBTC Network is the official Wrapped Bitcoin destination for an ERC-20 tokenized BTC asset on Ethereum; verify the canonical contract on Etherscan before connecting a wallet.

how.d: Open the official WBTC domain and compare the token contract before using any wallet prompt or third-party route.
For BTC to WBTC or redemption, follow the current supported merchant, exchange, or app path shown from the official destination.
Confirm network, recipient address, custody model, confirmations, and any provider fees before submitting a transaction.
Use Bitcoin and Ethereum explorers to confirm the BTC leg, WBTC mint, burn, or ERC-20 transfer status.

points.p: WBTC is an ERC-20 representation of Bitcoin used across Ethereum wallets, exchanges, and DeFi apps; the canonical contract is visible on Etherscan.
The WBTC model uses custodians and merchants for minting and burning, as described in the Wrapped Tokens whitepaper.
Cross-chain movement can introduce smart-contract, operator, custody, and user-error risks; Ethereum.org explains the main bridge trade-offs.

faq.q: What is a wbtc bridge used for?
faq.a: A wbtc bridge route is used to move between native BTC exposure and WBTC on Ethereum, or to move WBTC through supported cross-chain paths when available.

faq.q: Is wbtc bridge the same as wrapping Bitcoin?
faq.a: Not exactly. Wrapping BTC into WBTC uses a custodian and merchant model, while general bridging can also mean moving an already wrapped token across blockchain networks.

faq.q: What should I verify before using a wbtc bridge?
faq.a: Verify the official domain, token contract, connected network, recipient address, provider terms, and bridge risks before approving any transaction.

faq.q: Does the wbtc bridge have guaranteed fees or speed?
faq.a: No. Fees and timing depend on Bitcoin confirmations, Ethereum gas, the selected provider, and any merchant or exchange process; avoid pages claiming fixed returns or guaranteed settlement.

faq.q: Where is the official wbtc bridge app?
faq.a: Use the official WBTC destination at wbtc.network and follow the current app or merchant route shown there; do not trust lookalike domains.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify the domain before connecting a wallet. Educational content only, not financial advice.
